stdout:
[36m[Codex][0m Hold for Codex...


[36m[Codex][0m Hi, I’m Codex. I can summarize what was said and turn decisions into clear next steps.


[36m[Codex][0m Hi Alice, the last person-name I see mentioned was Priya: “Priya raised a concern about the approval policy.”


[36m[Codex][0m Main points from the last 5 minutes: rollout should be narrowed to 10%, the launch is still blocked on Codex parity, and the migration timeline is slipping. Repeated action items were to revoke the leaked token, check the byte ceiling behavior, confirm the marker-file crash path, pull up the GitHub issue, and coordinate with the mobile release branch.


[36m[Codex][0m Several people repeated the same point: “Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path.” It was often paired with sharing the dashboard link, pulling up the GitHub issue, revoking the leaked token, and coordinating with mobile release.


[36m[Codex][0m Speakers so far: Alice, Bob, Carol, Dan, and Eve.


[36m[Codex][0m 8 to 10 minutes ago, the discussion was mostly about rollout risk and blockers: Sentry auth alerts, revoking a leaked token, narrowing rollout to 10%, and the launch being blocked on Codex parity. There was also a recurring technical thread about marker file vs env var startup ordering, byte-ceiling retry behavior, and testing gaps from skipping a live Meet run.


[36m[Codex][0m Yes. Multiple people said “the launch is blocked on the codex parity work,” including Alice, Eve, Dan, Bob, and Carol.


[36m[Codex][0m The meeting focused on launch readiness and risk. Main themes: launch is blocked on Codex parity, rollout should likely narrow to 10%, the migration timeline is slipping, Sentry alerts are firing on the auth path, and a leaked token still needs revocation.


[36m[Codex][0m Technical threads: marker-file startup ordering was favored over env vars, byte-ceiling behavior needs checking to avoid repeated truncation retries, and live Meet testing is being skipped with known gaps around caption observation, meet.new resolution, and participant auto-leave. Other notes: prompt cache looked good, Sonnet coding benchmarks looked strong, wizard re-import is idempotent, QA found a flaky chat hardening test, and mobile release coordination is needed.


[36m[Codex][0m Carol said: “Postgres for prod SQLite for tests is the standard split — Mohammed pinged about the database pick — let me share the dashboard link.”
